methods.description: Process for measuring the δ 2 H and δ 18 O values of water samples using an isotopic water analyzer to understand isotopic composition deviations from the Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (V-SMOW).

steps.description: The δ 2 H and δ 18 O values of the water samples were measured using an isotopic water analyzer (Picarro, L2130-i) at the Key Laboratory of Western China's Environmental Systems, Ministry of Education, China.
steps.searchable_summary: measure δ 2 H and δ 18 O values using isotopic water analyzer

steps.description: The working standard water samples were added between every seven samples.
steps.searchable_summary: add working standard water samples between every seven samples

steps.description: All water sample was loaded in the isotope analyzer for isotope measurement by an injector with a 0.22 um filter membrane.
steps.searchable_summary: load water samples into isotope analyzer with 0.22 um filter

steps.description: Each sample was parallelly measured six times, and the last three results were used for data processing.
steps.searchable_summary: measure each sample six times and use last three results

steps.description: Results were expressed as parts per thousand deviations from the Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (V-SMOW).
steps.searchable_summary: express results as deviations from V-SMOW

steps.description: Deuterium excess (d-excess) for each water sample was calculated as d-excess = δ 2 H − 8·δ 18 O.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate d-excess as δ 2 H − 8·δ 18 O

steps.description: The calculation of amount-weighted average isotopic composition for a given period was calculated by the following equation: where P i is the precipitation amount. R i is the values of δ 18 O, δ 2 H and d-excess for sample i, and n is the total number of samples.
